title: What to know about Teucrium chamaedrys before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Teucrium chamaedrys are treating fever by virtue of its flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which possess antipyretic properties, and digestive issues due to its iridoid glycosides that exhibit anti-diarrheal and anti-inflammatory activities. It has also been used for cough relief as a result of its phenolic compounds that inhibit the production of mucus. Furthermore, Teucrium chamaedrys has been employed to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs. Additionally, the iridoid glycosides present in this plant have been used to treat rheumatism by reducing inflammation and pain. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic compounds also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions, arthritis, and inflammation.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Teucrium chamaedrys are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is mainly due to flavonoids such as luteolin and quercetin, that exhibit a wide range of inhibitory effects on bacteria and fungi. The plant also possesses antioxidant properties, which are mediated by phenolic compounds and ascorbic acid, that contribute to its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Moreover, its anti-inflammatory activity is associated with the presence of diterpenes, such as teucrin A, that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. This is further supported by its analgesic and antispasmodic effects, which are mediated by flavonoids and terpenoids, such as rosmarinic acid, that reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain. The plant's antibacterial and antiviral activities are also noteworthy, with volatile oils and flavonoids exhibiting inhibitory effects against a range of pathogens, including viruses and bacteria.
identification: Teucrium chamaedrys is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its woody base, square stems, and small, oval-shaped leaves with entire margins, typically 1-4 cm in length, and its flowers are purple, tubular, and borne in axillary whorls. This species is native to Europe, western Asia, and north Africa, and has been naturalized in other parts of the world, including North America and Australia. It thrives in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade, and can grow up to 30 cm in height. Teucrium chamaedrys is found in a variety of habitats, including rocky outcrops, grasslands, and open woods. Its flowers are typically in bloom from May to August, and are an important source of nectar for various pollinators.
cultivation: Teucrium chamaedrys is a perennial subshrub that thrives in full sun to partial shade, requiring well-drained, dry to moist soils with a slightly alkaline pH, and tolerates a range of temperatures from 10-30°C. It exhibits a bushy growth habit, typically reaching 10-30 cm in height and spreading 20-40 cm in width, and can be propagated through division, cuttings, or seed. For optimal growth, regular pruning is recommended to maintain shape and encourage new growth, and it's essential to avoid overwatering, which can lead to root rot. Teucrium chamaedrys also benefits from a layer of mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds, and it's recommended to repot every 2-3 years to maintain soil fertility. By providing the right conditions and care, Teucrium chamaedrys can be a low-maintenance and long-lasting addition to a garden or indoor space.
history: Teucrium chamaedrys is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, with documented evidence dating back to ancient Greece and Rome. The Greek physician Dioscorides (40-90 AD) mentioned its use to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin disorders. The plant's leaves were also used as a diuretic and to treat urinary problems. In medieval Europe, Teucrium chamaedrys was employed to treat wounds and skin conditions, with the leaves being applied topically to promote healing. Historical records also suggest that the plant was used to treat fever and as a general tonic to improve overall health.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a class of compounds known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Teucrium chamaedrys contains Saponins, which are a group of compounds with surface-active and detergent properties, having a foaming and emulsifying effect.

constituents.name: Rosmarinic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids, a constituent of Teucrium chamaedrys, possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids found in Teucrium chamaedrys have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides in Teucrium chamaedrys are bitter, astringent and have a sweet taste.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a group of compounds with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, and are also known for their ability to inhibit bacterial growth and induce apoptosis in cancer cells.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids, found in Teucrium chamaedrys, are a type of compound known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are a type of compound found in Teucrium chamaedrys that possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, also exhibiting proanthocyanidin-like activity.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent, bitter compounds with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that have been found in the plant Teucrium chamaedrys.

constituents.name: Rosmarinic Acid
constituents.description: A diterpenic carboxylic acid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Teucrium chamaedrys contains Alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: Luteolin is a flavonoid constituent of Teucrium chamaedrys, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Catechins
constituents.description: Catechins are flavanols with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and are known to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities.

constituents.name: Caffeic acid
constituents.description: Caffeic acid is a phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: A type of phytoestrogen found in Teucrium chamaedrys, having antioxidant and estrogen-like properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic Glycosides are a type of compound found in Teucrium chamaedrys that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Teucrin
constituents.description: Teucrin is a diterpenoid lactone with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Ingestion of Teucrium chamaedrys causes irritation to the gastrointestinal tract resulting in abdominal pain.
Teucrium chamaedrys ingestion causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to loose, watery stools.
Contact with Teucrium chamaedrys causes skin redness and inflammation.
Teucrium chamaedrys contains teucrin A, which causes headaches due to its spasmogenic action on the smooth muscle in the blood vessels of the brain
Teucrium chamaedrys ingestion causes physical exhaustion and lack of energy.
Some individuals experience skin irritation, itching, or hives in response to Teucrium chamaedrys contact.
Teucrium chamaedrys contains nepetalactone, which causes liver damage in humans and animals due to hepatotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids.

uses_list.description: This plant has been used to treat diarrhea due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea.
Its antipyretic properties make it useful in treating fever, as it helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.
The plant's anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to reduce pain and swelling make it useful in treating rheumatism, a condition characterized by inflammation and pain in the joints.
Its anti-inflammatory properties allow it to be used to treat inflammation, a common symptom of various conditions, by reducing redness, swelling, and pain in affected areas.
The plant's bronchodilatory properties make it useful in treating asthma, as it helps to relax the airway muscles and improve breathing.
Its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it useful in treating coughs, particularly those caused by respiratory tract infections.
The plant's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties make it useful in treating gastroenteritis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the stomach and intestines.
Its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties make it useful in treating eczema, a condition characterized by inflammation and skin lesions.

The plant's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties make it useful in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviating symptoms.
Its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it useful in treating bronchitis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the airways and mucus production.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including diarrhea, which it has been shown to alleviate through its anti-diarrheal properties. Its anti-inflammatory compounds have also been utilized to treat inflammation, a common symptom of gastroenteritis, a condition for which Teucrium chamaedrys has been found to be effective in reducing the severity of symptoms. Furthermore, its antipyretic properties make it useful in treating fever, which is often associated with rheumatism, a condition that the plant has been traditionally used to alleviate. Additionally, the plant has been found to be effective in treating asthma, digestive issues, wounds, dyspepsia, bronchitis, colic, cough, arthritis, gastritis, skin irritation, eczema, respiratory issues, migraine, gout, and skin infections, often by reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, or promoting wound healing. Its diverse range of therapeutic properties make Teucrium chamaedrys a valuable treatment option for a wide range of health conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, fever, and inflammation, which are often caused by gastrointestinal and respiratory problems. It is also used to alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, gastroenteritis, cough, and asthma, as well as gastritis, bronchitis, and sore throat, all of which are linked to impaired digestion and compromised immune function. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat eczema, skin irritation, influenza, and arthritis, all of which are associated with imbalances in the body's energy and circulation. Additionally, it is employed to treat colic, skin infections, respiratory issues, and vomiting, often in conjunction with other herbal remedies. Lastly, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to promote wound healing and prevent the onset of skin problems, such as eczema and skin infections, as well as respiratory issues like bronchitis and cough.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, which are often accompanied by inflammation and eczema. The plant's therapeutic properties also make it effective in treating cough suppression, asthma, and skin irritation. Additionally, it has been used to treat rheumatism, colic, and hiccups, while its antimicrobial properties also make it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and gastroenteritis. Furthermore, Teucrim chamaedrys has been found to be beneficial in treating bronchitis, cholera, and gout, which are often accompanied by jaundice. Its anti-inflammatory properties also make it effective in treating arthritis and other conditions such as gout.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to treat asthma, and its antispasmodic properties help alleviate respiratory issues. Additionally, it is often prescribed to alleviate diarrhea, fever, and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This herb has also been found to be effective in treating inflammation, cough, eczema, and dyspepsia by regulating digestion and reducing allergic reactions. Furthermore, its antidiarrheal and antispasmodic properties make it a popular remedy for digestive issues, gastroenteritis, and menstrual issues in women. The herb is also used to treat arthritis, gastritis, ulcers, diabetes, colic, skin irritation, pain, gout, sore throat, and other conditions due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Teucrium chamaedrys is used to treat fever, diarrhea and inflammation, often associated with rheumatism. It has also been used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, such as cough, and to aid in wound healing. Additionally, it may help with eczema, gastroenteritis, and digestive issues, as well as asthma and respiratory issues. The plant is sometimes used to address skin irritation, gastritis, and colic, while also providing relief from sore throat and respiratory problems. Furthermore, it is also used to treat arthritis, influenza, and malaria, showcasing its broad range of applications in homeopathic medicine.

The plant's ability to lower blood pressure is due to its high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to relax blood vessels, reduce vascular resistance, and improve blood flow, ultimately leading to a decrease in blood pressure.

This benefit is attributed to the plant's high content of essential oils, particularly thujone, which has been shown to stimulate digestion, improve gut motility, and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby increasing digestion and alleviating digestive disorders.

This benefit is attributed to the plant's high content of essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, which have been shown to have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, thereby fighting infections and promoting overall health.

The plant's ability to cure diarrhea is due to its high content of essential oils, particularly thujone, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, improve gut motility, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea.

This benefit is attributed to the plant's high content of flavonoids and essential oils, which have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increase the production of white blood cells, and reduce inflammation, thereby enhancing immunity and promoting overall health.

The plant's ability to treat arthritis is due to its high content of flavonoids, particularly ursolic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation, improve joint mobility, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis.

This benefit is attributed to the plant's high content of flavonoids, particularly ursolic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and reduce the risk of cancer development.

The plant's ability to support respiratory health is due to its high content of essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, which have been shown to have expectorant properties, reduce inflammation in the airways, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders.

benefits_description: Teucrium chamaedrys reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Lowering blood pressure is achieved through the plant's ability to relax blood vessels and reduce vascular resistance. The essential oil of Teucrium chamaedrys increases digestion by stimulating the release of digestive enzymes and improving gut motility. Relieving anxiety is facilitated by the plant's sedative and anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Teucrium chamaedrys fights infections and cures diarrhea by exhibiting antimicrobial and antidiarrheal properties due to its terpenoids and iridoids, while also enhancing immunity, treating arthritis, fighting cancer cells, and supporting respiratory health through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys reduce blood pressure through its vasodilatory properties, caused by the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. Lower cholesterol levels in the blood can be achieved due to its ability to inhibit the expression of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis. Teucrium chamaedrys also stimulate blood flow by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator, thereby improving circulation. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties help to prevent blood clots by inhibiting the activity of platelet activating factor, thereby reducing the risk of thrombosis. Furthermore, Teucrium chamaedrys regulate heart rate and stimulate blood flow, reduce inflammation, increase oxygenation, enhance endothelial function, and support cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which collectively contribute to its overall cardiovascular benefits.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the digestive system by reducing swelling and pain in the gut. This plant eases constipation by stimulating the movement of food through the digestive tract, thanks to its laxative properties that increase water and electrolyte absorption. Soothing digestive issues is also a key function, as Teucrium chamaedrys calms diarrhea by reducing the frequency and severity of watery stools, a result of its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria. Additionally, it treats bloating by breaking down gas and reducing pressure in the digestive system, a function attributed to its carminative properties. Furthermore, it heals ulcers by protecting the mucous membrane lining the stomach and small intestine, thanks to its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and promote tissue repair.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys regulates thyroid function through its flavonoids, which have been shown to stimulate thyroid hormone production. Stabilizes hormones in the body, thanks to its antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and lipid peroxidation. Activates metabolism by increasing the expression of genes involved in energy metabolism, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the body. Balances cortisol levels by modulating the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. This balance in cortisol levels, combined with its ability to suppress oxidation and maintain glucose homeostasis, also supports pancreas function, boosts adrenal function, reduces inflammation, and enhances insulin sensitivity.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Teucrium chamaedrys protects skin from damage by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which counteract the effects of free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. The plant reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory compounds, alleviating redness and swelling in the skin. Teucrium chamaedrys heals wounds by promoting tissue regeneration due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for healing. The plant stimulates regeneration by promoting cell proliferation and differentiation, facilitating the repair of damaged skin tissue. Additionally, Teucrium chamaedrys fights oxidation, sooths irritation, boosts immunity, cures infections, tightens pores, and calms eczema through its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys stimulates lymph flow by releasing compounds that increase blood flow and enhance the contraction of lymphatic vessels. Boosting immune response is achieved through the antimicrobial and antiviral properties of the plant, which inhibit the growth of pathogens and activate immune cells. Reducing inflammation quickly is a result of the anti-inflammatory compounds present in Teucrium chamaedrys, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Enhancing detoxification process is facilitated by the antioxidant properties of the plant, which neutralize free radicals and promote the excretion of toxins. Teucrium chamaedrys also supports tissue repair by promoting the synthesis of collagen and enhancing the expression of genes involved in tissue regeneration, while activating antioxidant defense, stabilizing cell membranes, increasing lymphatic efficiency, regulating fluid dynamics, and maintaining tissue health.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Teucrium chamaedrys reduces joint inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and neutralize free radicals that contribute to joint damage. Decreasing muscle pain is also a benefit of this plant, as its analgesic and anti-irritant properties help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate discomfort. Relaxing muscle tension is facilitated by the relaxant and sedative effects of Teucrium chamaedrys, which enable the muscles to relax and reduce muscle strain. Stimulating bone growth and preventing osteoporosis are achieved through the plant's osteogenic properties, which promote the formation of new bone tissue and increase bone density. Teucrium chamaedrys eases muscle fatigue by releasing pain chemicals that reduce muscle pain and spasms, while also supporting joint health by reducing arthritis symptoms and improving bone density.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys stabilizes nerve cells, exerting its effects through antioxidant properties that protect the integrity of neural membranes. This herb calms nervous system by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, contributing to a balanced physiological state. Additionally, Teucrium chamaedrys reduces anxiety symptoms through its anti-anxiolytic properties, which help to decrease stress-induced cortisol levels and promote relaxation. The plant's anti-spasmodic properties relax muscle tensions, while its anti-neurotoxic properties soothe nervous disorders, such as epilepsy and Parkinson's disease. Furthermore, Teucrium chamaedrys regulates nerve signals through its modulating effects on ion channels and receptors, improves cognitive function by enhancing neural connections, protects nerve tissues through its antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties, and prevents neurodegeneration by reducing oxidative stress and promoting neuronal health.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys regulates hormones by containing flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help maintain hormonal balance. This plant supports fertility due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which create a favorable environment for reproductive health. Teucrium chamaedrys improves libido through its ability to stimulate the release of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which are associated with sexual arousal. By stimulating ovulation, this plant enhances ovulation by promoting the growth and maturation of ovarian follicles, a process that is influenced by antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. Additionally, Teucrium chamaedrys increases testosterone levels and reduces infertility by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in testosterone production, while also toning the reproductive system and balancing menstrual cycles.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys reduce inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, while also ease congestion by acting as a natural expectorant. This plant prevent infections due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which boost immunity by stimulating the production of white blood cells. Teucrium chamaedrys loosen mucus by its mucolytic properties, which slow allergies by inhibiting the release of histamine. The plant alleviate coughs by soothing the irritations in the respiratory tract with its anti-spasmodic properties, and clear airways by relaxing bronchial muscles. Teucrium chamaedrys also kill bacteria with its bactericidal properties, and its antioxidant properties further contribute to its overall respiratory health benefits.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Teucrium chamaedrys prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties, which help protect the kidneys from oxidative stress and damage. Additionally, this plant reduces urine retention by its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract and promoting smooth urination. The antimicrobial properties of Teucrium chamaedrys protect bladder function by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and preventing infections. It also treats urinary tract infections by its antibacterial properties, which kill off bacteria and promote healing. Furthermore, the relaxing effect of Teucrium chamaedrys on urinary muscles boosts kidney function, alleviates nocturia symptoms, regulates urine output, heals bladder ulcers, and relieves urinary incontinence, all due to its antispasmodic and soothing properties that calm the urinary system.

properties_list.description: Teucrium chamaedrys has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids in the plant, demonstrating its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms.

The antioxidant activity of Teucrium chamaedrys has been attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, which are capable of neutralizing free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress in cells.

Teucrium chamaedrys has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds that can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory diseases.
The analgesic properties of Teucrium chamaedrys are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to have pain-relieving effects in various studies.
Teucrium chamaedrys has been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids that can relax smooth muscle and alleviate spasms.

The antibacterial properties of Teucrium chamaedrys are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids, which have been found to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and demonstrate potential as a natural antimicrobial agent.

parts_description: The most used parts of Teucrium chamaedrys for medicinal purposes are its leaves. The leaves of Teucrium chamaedrys contain flavonoids, diterpenes, and phenolic acids as primary bioactive compounds. These compounds contribute to the leaves' primary medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in the leaves may also exhibit hepatoprotective and cardiovascular protective effects, while the phenolic acids could provide antidiabetic and anticancer properties. The diterpenes found in the leaves may contribute to their antifeeding and antiparasitic activities.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and diterpenes, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, with primary health benefits including the treatment of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as the prevention of cardiovascular disease and cancer.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative and anti-anxiety properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys tincture is used to treat inflammation, skin issues, cold, wound healing, respiratory issues, cough, fever, gastrointestinal issues, diarrhea, and pain. It's a common herbal preparation, widely used in traditional medicine. One of its common effects is its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat infections. The tincture is made from the leaves and stems of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. The process of making the tincture is moderately difficult, requiring careful selection and preparation of the plant material, followed by a solvent extraction process to obtain the desired active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather a quantity of fresh or dried Teucrium chamaedrys leaves and stems, ensuring they are clean and free from contaminants.
Chop the gathered leaves and stems into smaller pieces to increase their surface area and facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped leaves and stems with a solvent, such as high-proof vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Steep the tincture mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to aid in the extraction process.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and minor burns, and as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys salve is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various skin issues, including burns, wounds, bruises, insect bites, skin irritation, and eczema. It is a widely used preparation in folk medicine. One of the common effects of applying Teucrium chamaedrys salve is its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The preparation is typically made from the leaves and roots of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant, which contains bioactive compounds with medicinal properties. However, creating the salve requires significant effort and expertise due to the complex process of extracting and purifying the active ingredients from the plant material.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves and roots of Teucrium chamaedrys, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the leaves and roots in a large pot and cover them with a neutral oil, such as olive or grapeseed oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for several hours, allowing the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Add a natural preservative, such as beeswax, to the oil and mix well, then pour the salve into small containers for storage.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys decoction is used to treat gastroenteritis, dyspepsia, diarrhea, colic, flatulence, colic in infants and indigestion. It's a common preparation in herbal medicine and has been used for centuries. This decoction typically has a soothing effect on the digestive system. It's commonly made from the leaves and roots of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant. Preparation of Teucrium chamaedrys decoction is moderately difficult, requiring careful measurement of ingredients and boiling to extract the active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves of Teucrium chamaedrys and its roots, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned leaves and roots in a heat-resistant container, making sure they are well covered with water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before storing it in the refrigerator for later use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used for treating fever, inflammation, and as a diuretic and expectorant to relieve coughs and bronchitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys tea is a preparation used to treat nausea, diarrhea, cough, inflammation, fever, gastroenteritis, rheumatism, and bronchitis. It's a very common herbal remedy, widely available and frequently used. This preparation often causes sweating. The tea is made by infusing the leaves and stems of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant in hot water. Preparation is relatively simple, as it only requires boiling the plant material and then straining the liquid.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried leaves and stems of Teucrium chamaedrys plant.
Rinse the leaves and stems under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the leaves and stems in a clean tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the leaves and stems in boiling water for an adequate amount of time.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then serve the herbal tea hot or cold.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Poultice made from this plant is used to treat wounds, skin infections, and eczema, and to reduce inflammation and pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys poultice is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various skin conditions and injuries, including burns, boils, wounds, bruises, insect bites, cuts and rashes. It is a commonly used preparation in folk medicine, often employed to reduce fever and alleviate the symptoms of certain skin rashes. The application of this poultice typically results in a significant reduction in inflammation and pain. This preparation is made by steeping the leaves and roots of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant in a solvent, such as water or oil, to release their active compounds. The process of making a Teucrium chamaedrys poultice is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal preparation techniques and access to the necessary equipment and ingredients.
preparation_recipe: Gather a sufficient amount of fresh Teucrium chamaedrys leaves and roots.
Rinse the leaves and roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves and roots into small pieces and mix them together in a bowl.
Soak the chopped mixture in a small amount of water for a few minutes to soften it.
Strain the liquid from the mixture and apply the remaining soft mixture directly to the affected skin area as a poultice.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Ointment made from this plant is used topically to treat wounds, skin irritations, and insect bites.
preparations.preparation_overview: Teucrium chamaedrys ointment is used to treat wounds, burns, bruises, boils, insect bites, eczema, skin irritation, abscesses, fungal infections. It's a common preparation in herbal medicine, widely available and employed by many practitioners. Its effects include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and promoting healing in various skin conditions. The ointment is typically made from the leaves and stems of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant, which are harvested, dried, and then infused or extracted to create the medicinal preparation. The process of creating Teucrium chamaedrys ointment is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbalism and preparation techniques, but the end result is a valuable tool for treating a range of skin issues.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh leaves and stems of Teucrium chamaedrys in the wild or in a controlled environment.
Wash the leaves and stems thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the leaves and stems using a clean towel or by air-drying them in a cool, shaded area.
Grind the dried leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to create a smooth and consistent ointment.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Teucrium chamaedrys are experiences nausea, shows abdominal pain, exhibits diarrhea, and develops vomiting, which can be caused by consuming large quantities of the plant. Experiencing dizziness and developing headaches may also occur due to the plant's toxic compounds, such as teucrin and teucrin B. Manifests skin irritation is a possible side effect, especially in individuals with sensitive skin. Shows fatigue and exhibits allergic reactions are also potential side effects, which can be triggered by the plant's volatile oils and alkaloids. Manifests liver damage is a severe and potentially life-threatening side effect that can occur if Teucrium chamaedrys is ingested in large amounts or for extended periods.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Teucrium chamaedrys may interact with certain medications and exacerbate medical conditions, such as kidney problems, requiring medical supervision.
Teucrium chamaedrys essential oil is toxic to the developing fetus and should be avoided during pregnancy and breastfeeding.
Allergic contact dermatitis has been reported in individuals sensitive to Teucrium chamaedrys.
Skin contact causes irritation due to allergenic sap
Direct eye contact with Teucrium chamaedrys sap causes phototoxicity and eye damage
High doses of Teucrium chamaedrys can cause liver damage and gastrointestinal issues, necessitating careful monitoring.
Teucrium chamaedrys may interact with medications such as antacids, digoxin, and cyclosporine, decreasing their effectiveness or increasing the risk of side effects.
Teucrium chamaedrys exacerbates conditions involving pregnancy, breastfeeding, and allergies.
Prolonged use of Teucrium chamaedrys may lead to liver toxicity.

precautions.precaution_name: Quality and purity of product must be guaranteed
precautions.precaution_list_description: Teucrium chamaedrys extracts are subject to adulteration; verify authenticity and chemical composition through laboratory testing.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Natural product research", Teucrium chamaedrys has shown potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable herb for medicinal purposes. Additionally, it has been used in traditional medicine to address digestive problems and diabetes. The study also highlights the need for further research to confirm the therapeutic properties of Teucrium chamaedrys and compare it with other species.

According to a study published by "Natural product research", Teucrium chamaedrys has been found to contain various bioactive compounds, including teucrin A, dihydroteugin, and teucrioside, which are isolated from its aerial parts. The study suggests that these compounds may exhibit potential medicinal properties, but more research is needed to confirm their effects. The isolation of these compounds from Teucrium chamaedrys highlights the plant's potential as a source of new and useful medicinal agents.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Teucrium chamaedrys, a plant commonly used in Kurdish traditional medicine, has been found to be a natural source of specialized metabolites that are potentially beneficial to human health. These metabolites may offer protection against various health issues, such as jaundice and abdominal problems. The bioactive compounds present in Teucrium chamaedrys may provide relief from stomachache and other gastrointestinal disorders, highlighting its potential as a valuable herbal remedy.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Teucrium chamaedrys are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. This plant also exhibits significant antioxidant activity, thanks to its richness in phenolic compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Teucrium chamaedrys displays anti-inflammatory properties, primarily due to the presence of diterpenes, like chamaedrysine, that modulate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Furthermore, the plant's analgesic and antispasmodic properties are attributed to the presence of volatile oils, such as borneol and camphor, that exert a relaxing effect on the nervous system, providing relief from pain and muscle spasms. Additionally, the antibacterial activity of Teucrium chamaedrys is responsible for its ability to inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, thereby preventing infections and promoting wound healing.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Teucrium chamaedrys are flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Saponins present in the plant have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, contributing to its healing effects. Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic acid, has been demonstrated to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Triterpenoids, specifically ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, supporting the plant's healing potential. These constituents work synergistically to exhibit the plant's therapeutic effects, highlighting the complex and multifaceted nature of Teucrium chamaedrys' healing properties.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Teucrium chamaedrys for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to their expectorant properties. A salve is another preparation derived from the plant, which is applied topically to soothe wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Decoctions are typically used to treat digestive issues, including stomach ulcers and diarrhea, as they help to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. Tea preparations are also valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, and are often consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and headaches. A poultice made from the plant's leaves can be applied directly to the skin to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, while an ointment is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Teucrium chamaedrys medicinally are consulting a doctor before use, especially if you have any underlying health conditions, as excessive consumption can be detrimental. Pregnancy and breastfeeding women should exercise caution, as the effects on the fetus or infant are not well-documented. Allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or digestive issues, are possible, and users should monitor their body for any adverse reactions. Skin contact can cause irritation, and users should wear protective clothing when handling the plant, while eye contact can cause damage, requiring immediate flushing with water. Additionally, dosage must be carefully monitored, and interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, are possible, potentially exacerbating conditions like hypertension or diabetes, and quality and purity of the product must be guaranteed to avoid contamination or adulteration.
intro_description: Teucrium chamaedrys, commonly known as woodland germander, is a medicinal herb traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and anxiety-related disorders. The main health benefits associated with this herb include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herb's therapeutic properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which help to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive compounds present in Teucrium chamaedrys include diterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation.
